title: scirpus umbellatus
intro: Scirpus umbellatus, also known as umbrella sedge or umbrella club-rush, is a type of perennial aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Scirpus umbellatus are thought to include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions or decoctions, and it can also be dried and used as a tea or added to salves and ointments.
